tracks.name: Start/Main BGM
tracks.note: International version uses noise channel percussion; Japanese version uses triangle channel bass with sustained background note

tracks.name: Larios (Boss Theme)
tracks.note: International version extended by 4 bars in first and third sections; uses two pulse channels and triangle; Japanese version uses only triangle channel

tracks.name: Area Target
tracks.note: International version adds noise channel percussion

tracks.name: Area Clear
tracks.note: International version plays immediately after target destruction; Japanese version preceded by jingle

tracks.name: Purser (Power-Up)
tracks.note: International version adds noise channel percussion

tracks.name: Extend (1-Up Jingle)
tracks.note: International version plays at higher key than Japanese version

tracks.name: Bonus (Larios Defeat)
tracks.note: International version only; absent from Japanese version

tracks.name: Cleopatra (Congratulation)
tracks.note: Plays after destroying Cleopatra target and Area Omega

tracks.name: Game Over
tracks.note: International version uses unused track from arcade original; Japanese version lacks Game Over theme

distinctive_elements: Noise channel percussion added in International version for Main BGM, Purser, Target, and Clear tracks
Japanese version exclusively uses noise channel for sound effects only
Creative use of pitch bends in pulse channels
Staccato pulse channel patterns in Main BGM
Extended boss themes in International version for closer arcade accuracy

regional_differences.japanese_version: Straight arrangement of arcade original; uses triangle channel bass with sustained background notes; lacks noise channel in music; includes Area Target jingle before Clear theme
regional_differences.international_version: Keiji Yamagishi arrangements; adds noise channel percussion; uses creative pulse channel patterns and pitch bends; excludes Area Target jingle; includes Game Over theme from unused arcade track; extended boss themes; higher pitch on Start and Extend tracks

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Star Force (NES)
